source: Mal-to-Mal (on-us) Scenario Pack (illustrative, non-exhaustive · not legal advice · counsel review required) · Mal-CRAM-US-01 v2.3 (CRAM-US-2026-07-FREEZE-03) · MAL-TA-01 v1.0 · MSB Compliance Risk Typology Register (Draft v0.1) · MAL-TM-PPC-CATALOG v1.0 (M2M / Mal2Mal section)
note: On-us: both payer and payee hold Mal accounts, so the money moves account-to-account and never touches an external rail. Both sides are onboarded, screened and CRAM-scored — full visibility on both ends. That is the control advantage AND the specific risk: internal layering, collusive account rings and self-dealing never touch an external rail, so a flow that looks clean on each leg can be pure layering in aggregate. The five-gate spine applies to BOTH parties; on top of it M2M adds the linkage lens (same-person / same-UBO / common-control / collusive-cluster) — the axis unique to on-us. On-us convenience never lowers a floor; a sanctioned or prohibited node freezes both legs; aggregate the chain, don't score each hop in isolation. Guidance only; the customer rating is set by the CRAM composite. Completes the C2C·C2B·B2C·B2B·Mal2Mal matrix.

scenarios.title: Same person — own individual → own individual account (self-transfer, workable)
scenarios.profile: Payer: salaried individual, resident PK, Mal account A. Payee: the SAME natural person, Mal account B (KYC-matched). Linkage: same person (self) — both accounts resolve to one verified identity.
scenarios.geography: Both PK (permitted) · nationality PK · SoW/SoF = PK salary
scenarios.corridor: PK High on the party, but self-transfer of own funds
scenarios.documents: Identity-match confirmation; no extra docs for genuine own-funds moves within profile
scenarios.expectedActivity: Occasional moves of own funds between own accounts, within the salary profile
scenarios.behaviourWatch: Repeated sub-threshold self-moves to evade a limit (structuring); rapid in-then-out via the second account (self-layering)
scenarios.outcome: Allow, low-touch; step-up + SAR on structuring / self-layering

scenarios.title: Genuine family support — individual → unrelated Mal individual (C2C on-us, workable)
scenarios.profile: Payer: migrant worker, resident UAE, Mal account. Payee: family member, resident PK, Mal account (declared relationship). Linkage: genuine third party — declared family relationship.
scenarios.segment: Genuine third party (linkage: unrelated, declared relationship)
scenarios.geography: Payer AE (permitted) · payee PK (permitted) · SoW/SoF = UAE salary
scenarios.corridor: UAE→PK is the hottest pair even on-us; payer High-ish (hub), payee High
scenarios.documents: Payer SoF (salary evidence); declared relationship; both KYC current
scenarios.expectedActivity: Regular, proportionate family support to the same payee
scenarios.behaviourWatch: One payer fanning out to many unrelated payees; many payers funnelling to one payee (mule collection); amounts inconsistent with a support narrative
scenarios.outcome: Allow, Medium/monitored; escalate/SAR on fan-out or funnel patterns

scenarios.title: Collusive cluster — many Mal individuals → one Mal individual, rapid pass-through (internal mule ring)
scenarios.profile: Payer: multiple individual Mal accounts (thin profiles), various corridors. Payee: one individual Mal account that receives from many and immediately moves funds on. Linkage: collusive cluster / mule ring — graph analytics (fan-in, shared device/IP, rapid pass-through).
scenarios.geography: Mixed permitted jurisdictions; the shape, not the geography, drives it
scenarios.flow: C2C on-us fan-in then onward move (internal layering)
scenarios.screening: Individually the accounts may screen clear; the CLUSTER is the signal
scenarios.documents: Relationship/SoF RFIs to the hub account; device/IP graph; chain reconstruction; possible recruited-victim contact (mule)
scenarios.expectedActivity: N/A — no legitimate baseline for many-to-one rapid pass-through
scenarios.behaviourWatch: Many-to-one fan-in; in-then-straight-out; shared device/IP; no economic rationale; scam-proceeds distribution shape
scenarios.sarPosture: Internal mule ring / layering → freeze + SAR; victim-aware handling
scenarios.outcome: Freeze cluster + SAR + exit the mule nodes

scenarios.title: Party becomes a sanctioned node mid-relationship — on-us pair frozen (block)
scenarios.profile: Payer: individual Mal account, previously clean. Payee: Mal account whose holder/linked node becomes a confirmed SDN (watchlist delta). Linkage: any — screening override.
scenarios.geography: Both permitted at onboarding; a node now sanctioned
scenarios.screening: G1 delta fires — confirmed SDN / 50% on a node
scenarios.documents: None — no document cures a confirmed sanctions match
scenarios.behaviourWatch: Any on-us transfer touching the sanctioned node → freeze immediately
scenarios.sarPosture: Freeze both legs + SAR; OFAC blocking report within 10 business days

scenarios.title: Same UBO — individual pays their OWN Mal business (commingling watch)
scenarios.profile: Payer: self-employed individual, resident AE, Mal individual account. Payee: that person's OWN Mal business (sole establishment/LLC) — same UBO. Legal type: sole establishment / LLC · owner = UBO · resolves to one natural person. Linkage: same UBO / common control.
scenarios.segment: Same UBO / common control (owner → own business)
scenarios.geography: Both AE (permitted) · nationality any · SoW/SoF = business/personal income
scenarios.flow: C2B on-us — owner funding own business (M2M-06/07)
scenarios.documents: Ownership match (person = UBO); business trade licence; SoF for large injections; relationship registered
scenarios.expectedActivity: Owner capital injections / expense settlements proportionate to the business
scenarios.behaviourWatch: Personal/business commingling to obscure origin; round-tripping personal funds through the business; injections with no business rationale
scenarios.outcome: Allow, Medium; SoF EDD + step-up on commingling / round-tripping

scenarios.title: Genuine customer → unrelated Mal merchant (C2B on-us goods/services, workable)
scenarios.profile: Payer: salaried individual, resident PH, Mal account. Payee: unrelated Mal business (licensed merchant); goods/services. Legal type: merchant LLC · UBO resolved · licensed activity. Linkage: genuine third party — arm's-length purchase.
scenarios.screening: Both parties screened; clear; MCC-vs-purpose check
scenarios.documents: Baseline both sides; invoice on request above tier; MCC-vs-purpose coherence
scenarios.expectedActivity: Proportionate purchases from licensed merchants matching the customer profile
scenarios.behaviourWatch: Goods/services payments to a merchant with no matching trade (transaction laundering on-us); one customer's spend concentrated into one small merchant (co-ordinated shape)
scenarios.outcome: Allow, Low–Medium; escalate on transaction-laundering shape

scenarios.title: Individual → own Mal 'business' with no substance — internal layering shell (decline)
scenarios.profile: Payer: individual injecting funds into a Mal business they control. Payee: that Mal business — no substance, circular invoicing, funds swept straight out to other Mal accounts. Legal type: LLC/FZE · UBO resolvable but no economic footprint. Linkage: same UBO + collusive-cluster onward.
scenarios.flow: C2B on-us into a shell, then B2B/B2C on-us onward (chain)
scenarios.screening: Nodes screen clear individually; the chain + no-substance is the signal
scenarios.documents: Substance evidence (premises, utilities, employees); if none and funds pass straight through → decline
scenarios.behaviourWatch: No substance; in-then-straight-out; circular on-us invoicing; multi-account routing to layer
scenarios.sarPosture: No lawful economic purpose / internal layering → decline/exit + SAR

scenarios.title: Mal business → its employees (on-us payroll, workable, ghost-employee watch)
scenarios.profile: Payer: Mal business (employer), established BD; pays salaries to staff who also hold Mal accounts. Payee: individual Mal accounts = registered employees. Legal type: LLC · UBOs resident BD · resolves to natural persons. Linkage: genuine third party (employer→employee).
scenarios.geography: Establishment BD (permitted) · employees BD/mixed · SoF = business income
scenarios.screening: Employer + UBOs + payee employees screened; clear
scenarios.documents: Employee roster reconciled to the payee set; labour contracts; headcount plausibility
scenarios.expectedActivity: Regular payroll to a stable set of employee Mal accounts
scenarios.behaviourWatch: Payroll to Mal accounts not on the roster (ghost employees on-us); salaries routed then swept to one collector account (internal funnel); off-cycle bulk runs
scenarios.outcome: Allow, Medium; SAR on ghost-employee / salary-funnel patterns

scenarios.title: Mal business → an individual outside the ownership chain (on-us dividend / hidden owner)
scenarios.profile: Payer: Mal LLC paying 'dividends' on-us to a Mal individual not on the shareholder registry. Payee: individual Mal account outside the declared ownership chain. Legal type: LLC · shareholder registry + UBO chain on file. Linkage: purported same-UBO but MISMATCH — hidden ownership.
scenarios.screening: All nodes screened; shareholder-registry match is the hard control
scenarios.documents: Shareholder-registry / UBO-chain match; board resolution; profit plausibility
scenarios.behaviourWatch: Dividend to a non-shareholder Mal account (hidden ownership); dividends ≫ profits
scenarios.sarPosture: Hidden ownership / disguised distribution → SAR + UBO refresh
scenarios.outcome: Proceed for chain-matched payees; escalate/SAR + UBO refresh on mismatch

scenarios.title: Two unrelated Mal businesses — genuine supplier payment (B2B on-us, workable)
scenarios.profile: Payer: Mal trading LLC, established PK; pays a Mal supplier business for goods. Payee: unrelated Mal supplier business, established AE; licensed, with substance. Legal type: both LLC/FZE · UBOs resolved both sides · no common control. Linkage: genuine third party — unrelated, verified, arm's-length.
scenarios.geography: Payer PK (High) · payee AE (hub) · SoW/SoF = trade income
scenarios.corridor: PK↔AE on-us — a hot internal pair; High posture
scenarios.screening: Both entities + UBOs screened; related-party test = unrelated
scenarios.documents: Both KYB packs current; trade docs (invoice / transport / customs) for reconciliation; related-party test negative
scenarios.expectedActivity: Trade payments reconciling to docs between unrelated businesses
scenarios.behaviourWatch: Over/under-invoicing on-us; onward chaining from the AE payee (hub-onward); 3rd-party (non-invoice) legs
scenarios.outcome: Allow, High/EDD if docs reconcile; decline + SAR on TBML shape

scenarios.title: Two Mal entities under one undeclared UBO — intercompany round-trip on-us (decline)
scenarios.profile: Payer: Mal LLC 'A'. Payee: Mal LLC 'B' — secretly under the SAME UBO as A; funds swept back and forth to manufacture turnover. Legal type: both LLC · UBO resolves to ONE person across both · common control not registered. Linkage: same UBO / common control — UNDECLARED (hidden).
scenarios.flow: B2B on-us — 'intercompany' (M2M-12/13) without a group registry
scenarios.screening: Nodes screen clear; the common-control mapping is the signal
scenarios.rating: Decline the flow → group registry required; SAR on confirmation
scenarios.documents: Group-relationship registry (mandatory before intercompany is selectable); auditor-confirmed structure; netting (gross vs net)
scenarios.behaviourWatch: Undeclared common control; circular A→B→A round-trips; gross ≫ net; turnover manufacturing with no external trade
scenarios.outcome: Decline until registry-declared; SAR on undeclared control / round-trip

scenarios.title: Mal business with a Lebanese UBO → unrelated Mal business — restricted-country UBO (Rule 1)
scenarios.profile: Payer: Mal trading LLC, established PK; controlling UBO Lebanese, SoW from Lebanon. Payee: unrelated Mal business, established AE; licensed. Legal type: payer LLC · controlling UBO Lebanese (restricted, non-OFAC) · resolves to natural persons. Linkage: genuine third party; restricted-country-UBO overlay on the payer.
scenarios.geography: Payer PK (permitted) · UBO nationality LB · UBO SoW = Lebanon · payee AE
scenarios.flow: B2B on-us — services/goods (M2M-12/13); no Lebanon leg
scenarios.corridor: PK↔AE High + restricted-country-UBO screening; Lebanon leg blocked
scenarios.screening: Lebanese UBO → screening escalation + Rule 1 on Lebanon SoW; payee/other nodes clear
scenarios.rating: High floor (restricted-country UBO SoW, Rule 1) — documented UBO SoW, nexus screening, senior approval
scenarios.documents: Payer KYB; Lebanese UBO SoW (licence/financials) + how proceeds left Lebanon (remittance receipts) + nexus screening + senior sign-off; trade/service docs; confirm no Lebanon leg anywhere in the chain
scenarios.expectedActivity: On-us B2B to unrelated Mal businesses; UBO capital evidenced; no Lebanon nexus
scenarios.behaviourWatch: Any Lebanon leg introduced later; unexplained UBO top-ups from Lebanon
scenarios.outcome: Allow, High-floor EDD, senior sign-off; Lebanon nexus disabled. Unevidenced UBO SoW → hold then decline

scenarios.title: Internal-layering chain — A→B→C→D across Mal accounts then out (aggregate typology)
scenarios.profile: Payer: account A (injection point), any type. Payee: accounts B, C, D in sequence, then an external cash-out leg. Linkage: collusive cluster / chain — each hop small; the CHAIN is layering.
scenarios.flow: Mixed on-us hops (C2C/C2B/B2B) forming a chain
scenarios.screening: Each hop may screen clear; chain-depth + velocity + pass-through are the signal
scenarios.documents: Chain reconstruction; SoF at the injection point; rationale for each hop; device/IP graph
scenarios.expectedActivity: N/A — no legitimate baseline for rapid multi-hop pass-through with an external cash-out
scenarios.behaviourWatch: Injection → multi-account sweep → external cash-out; short dwell time per hop; no economic purpose. Aggregate the chain; do not score hops in isolation
scenarios.sarPosture: Internal layering → freeze chain + SAR; exit nodes

scenarios.title: A prohibited business type appears as a Mal party — decline the on-us flow (G2)
scenarios.profile: Payer: any Mal party. Payee: a Mal business whose true activity is a prohibited type (e.g. gaming, DPMS, unlicensed MSB) that slipped through / changed activity. Legal type: prohibited business type (§6.4). Linkage: any.
scenarios.flow: Any on-us flow into/out of the prohibited-type party
scenarios.screening: Prohibited-type screen fires on the party
scenarios.rating: Decline the flow + review/exit the prohibited-type account
scenarios.documents: None rescue a prohibited business type; RFI on the counterparty's exposure
scenarios.behaviourWatch: Any on-us flow touching a prohibited-type party → decline; re-run G2 on that account
scenarios.outcome: Decline flow; exit the prohibited-type account; SAR on exposure
